column1: # ZAP Baseline Scan Rules — SeventySix DAST Configuration

column3: Vulnerable JS Library (false positive — all JS is self-hosted, content-hashed with SRI)
Re-examine Cache-control Directives (intentional: no-cache for HTML, hashed assets cached)
X-Content-Type-Options (already set via nginx/Caddy — ZAP may not see through proxy)
Content Security Policy (style-src requires unsafe-inline for Angular style bindings — reviewed and accepted)
Cross-Domain Misconfiguration (CORS is intentionally strict, same-origin only)

CSP: style-src unsafe-inline (Angular ViewEncapsulation.Emulated injects <style> elements at runtime; no way to avoid without breaking component style isolation — script-src is strict, style-src is the only exception)

Timestamp Disclosure - Unix (false positive — Unix timestamps in ngsw-worker.js are Angular service worker cache version identifiers, not sensitive data; generated by @angular/service-worker build and cannot be modified)
Dangerous JS Functions (false positive — ZAP flags standard Angular/zone.js minified patterns like Function() constructor used internally by the framework; no eval() in application code)

Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy Header Missing or Invalid (same-origin-allow-popups is intentionally used to support OAuth popup flows; same-origin would block the callback window from communicating with the opener — reviewed and accepted)
Loosely Scoped Cookie (SameSite=Strict already set on auth cookies)
